twitter_posts.title: 🤯 And it worked! It made a basic iOS app in full Swift then made a screenshot and sent me a link to check it! It made it on my Hetzner VPS but then SSH'd into a @macincloud Mac Mini in a datacenter so
twitter_posts.description:
🤯 And it worked!

It made a basic iOS app in full Swift then made a screenshot and sent me a link to check it!

It made it on my Hetzner VPS but then SSH'd into a @macincloud Mac Mini in a datacenter somewhere to then build an app with Xcode on there

Very basic but a great start
twitter_posts.author: Pieter Levels

twitter_posts.title: Introducing AI Browser Games! Watch open & closed models build small browser games head to head. Open models like Kimi K2.7 were faster, cheaper, & produced games similar to Opus 4.8. In some cases, o
twitter_posts.description:
Introducing AI Browser Games!

Watch open & closed models build small browser games head to head.

Open models like Kimi K2.7 were faster, cheaper, & produced games similar to Opus 4.8. In some cases, open models were ~20x cheaper than closed ones too!

Video
twitter_posts.author: Hassan El Mghari

twitter_posts.title: Turned 28 this week! 🎉 Biggest goals for next year: ◆ Build bigger & more impressive open source AI apps & skills ◆ Do more education around OSS models, inference, & AI engineering ◆ Continue building
twitter_posts.description:
Turned 28 this week! 🎉

Biggest goals for next year:

◆ Build bigger & more impressive open source AI apps & skills
◆ Do more education around OSS models, inference, & AI engineering
◆ Continue building a world class Developer Experience team at Together

Onwards 🫡
twitter_posts.author: Hassan El Mghari

twitter_posts.author: Anthropic

twitter_posts.title: R to @AnthropicAI: The J-space lets us read, audit, and shape what Claude is actively thinking about—useful tools for keeping models trustworthy as they grow more capable. And it suggests surprising p
twitter_posts.description:
The J-space lets us read, audit, and shape what Claude is actively thinking about—useful tools for keeping models trustworthy as they grow more capable. And it suggests surprising parallels between language models and our own minds.

Read the full paper: transformer-circuits.pub/

articles.title: Another little thing that nothing but Fable could have helped me make today: You can now drag and drop files from your own computer into Windows 3.11 on http://pieter.com The file is saved inside C:\D
articles.description:
Another little thing that nothing but Fable could have helped me make today:

You can now drag and drop files from your own computer into Windows 3.11 on pieter.com

The file is saved inside C:\DOCS and if you want to share it with other people you can put it in C:\SHARED

It all
